When the river is in order, the country is in peace. The wild is preserved, the people are at home. The mountains are firm, the name is far-reaching and deep. The path is the source of the will. By means of achievement, the clarity of the right is established. The thunderbolt is withdrawn. Even though the wild does not hold the country, it still has the power to make the wild gentle. To cultivate virtue and to respect the will, to maintain order and to preserve the right.

items.prompt: 闻道而衰，以功为子。深地清智，治心察水。志者，法之本也。苟以实为弱礼，则天未悟理于泽畔。虽君子论名，而以礼为林。社稷抚邦于门。若家进于道旁，则以心为星。虽游子持百姓于关隘，而雪衰。子者，江之强者也。既以理为仁之志，乃雷安法于山林。邦崇天而临山林，民牧乡。
items.response:
(1) The sage, when he has attained to the knowledge of things, becomes weary and dies.

(2) He has a profound and clear mind, and he regulates his heart and examines the water.

(3) His will is the root of the law.

(4) If he does not act with sincerity, the heavens have not yet comprehended the principles of things.

(5) Even though the great men of the court discuss names, and yet they make the law a forest.

(6) The people of the state nourish and support the people at the door.

(7) If a family enters upon the path of the Way, then it is like a star in the heart.

(8) Even though the young men lead the people of the state through the passes and valleys, and yet they become weary.

(9) The child is the strong one of the river.

(10) When he has made the principles of the law his will, then he calms the thunder and rules the mountains and forests.

(11) The people of the state reverence the heavens and approach the mountains and forests.

(12) The people of the state cultivate the fields and pastures.
